sanskrit: ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमसः परमुच्यते । ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम् ॥
hindiTranslation: वह परमात्मा सम्पूर्ण ज्योतियोंका भी ज्योति और अज्ञानसे अत्यन्त परे कहा गया है ।  वह ज्ञानस्वरूप, जाननेयोग्य, ज्ञानसाधनसमुदाय से प्राप्त करनेयोग्य और सबके हृदयमें विराजमान है ॥
englishTranslation: It is the light of all lights, beyond darkness. It is knowledge, the knowable, reached through knowledge, and abiding in every heart.
essence: What illumines everything is already nearest to you.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The supreme reality is brighter than all light, beyond ignorance, and present in every heart. It is both what is known and the means of knowing it.
simpleInsight: You are not searching in the dark for something absent; the deepest clarity is already near.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen and Krishna is revealing the nature of the field, the knower, and the ultimate reality. Here Krishna says that the highest reality is the light of all lights, beyond darkness, and present in every heart.
modernRelevance: You sit in a silent room after a painful argument, replaying every word and wondering what is true. The answer is not somewhere far away; clarity is already closer than your panic.
translationLiteral: ज्योतिषाम् (of lights) / अपि (even) / तत् (that) / ज्योतिः (light) / तमसः (of darkness) / परम् (beyond) / उच्यते (is said) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / ज्ञेयम् (the knowable) / ज्ञानगम्यम् (reached through knowledge) / हृदि (in the heart) / सर्वस्य (of all) / विष्ठितम् (abiding)
insight: It shifts the mind from chasing truth outside to recognising the source of knowing within experience itself.

quoteVariants.text: The light behind every light lives in every heart.
Light of all lights, within all hearts.
What you seek already shines inside everything.
